target: ISS-2328 / HOLISTIC-HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2 hole 5 (moderate) — consolidate Sydney–Perth redistribution. Drop the explicit 4,352 / 3,893 / 460 km internal-disagreement numbers from part6 §6.10 STEELMAN-01 and part7 §7.1 body prose, replacing with cross-references to part2 §2.9 (the canonical V13 coordinate-system loop analysis). Part2 §2.9 confirmed canonical (no change). Part5 §5.0 left verbose per curmudgeon (different question: post-c1 author-demotion action). Part7 §7.1 TLDR is left untouched — see anticipated objections.
kernel_of_truth: Each of the three current locations (§2.9, part6 STEELMAN-01, part7 §7.1) has a legitimate analytical anchor for the Sydney–Perth case. §2.9 is the canonical V13-self-referential-loop analysis: the 4,352 vs 3,893 km internal disagreement is *the* concrete in-model contradiction the entire section turns on, and the arithmetic belongs there. STEELMAN-01 in part6 must restate the dome's 4,352 km Indian-Pacific-railway claim because that is the specific top-tier steelman the dome publishes — engaging with it without the number would be ducking the dome's own framing. Part7 §7.1 is genuinely about southern-hemisphere distance accuracy and uses Sydney–Perth as a calibration anchor for the out-of-sample SYD-EZE failure analysis. The redundancy critique is not that any of these is misplaced — it is that the *internal-disagreement arithmetic* (3,893 vs 4,352, delta 460 km) is the same evidence used three times in three frames, when one canonical statement plus two cross-references would carry the same argumentative weight without inviting a defender to claim we are padding our case.
why_it_doesnt_save_claim_for_dome_defend: Consolidating the arithmetic to §2.9 does not weaken any of the three sections' independent arguments. STEELMAN-01 still engages with the dome's 4,352 km Indian-Pacific-railway claim head-on; the consolidation only removes the second-mention scaffold contradiction that §2.9 already documents and that the existing cross-reference at the end of STEELMAN-01's 'why it fails' paragraph already points to. Part7 §7.1's primary thrust is SYD-EZE out-of-sample failure (78% V12, 8.4% V13 via three new free parameters and an unpublished n(r)) and the rim-circumnavigation 8–9× discrepancy — the internal-disagreement arithmetic is a supplementary sentence that §2.9 already covers; the existing cross-reference at the end of §7.1's third paragraph carries the consolidation cleanly. The argumentative weight does not move; only the re-derivation moves. A defender skimming three sections used to see the same delta-460 contradiction three times and could plausibly argue 'one good point dressed as three'; after consolidation the defender sees the arithmetic once in §2.9, with two narrowly-framed mentions in STEELMAN-01 and §7.1 that point back. The kernel-of-truth move is even more visible: §2.9 *is* the right home for this number, and we are acting on that judgment cleanly.

patches.find_text: Meanwhile, the dome's own coordinate scaffold gives 3,893 km for the same Sydney–Perth pair — disagreeing with the Finsler formula by 460 km within the same model.
patches.replace_text: Meanwhile, the dome's own coordinate scaffold (MDS on road distances) disagrees with the Finsler formula on the same Sydney–Perth pair within the dome's own model.
patches.rationale: Drops the redundant 3,893 / 460 km arithmetic in part6 §6.10 STEELMAN-01's 'why it fails' paragraph. The existing cross-reference in the very next sentence ('See Part 5, Test 1 and Section 2.9...') already routes the reader to §2.9 for the full internal-disagreement arithmetic, so no new anchor link is required. Keeps the structural claim (the scaffold and the Finsler formula disagree within the dome's own model) because that is the argumentative load-bearing point; defers the specific numbers to §2.9 where they live canonically.

patches.find_text: gives Sydney-Perth as 3,893 km while the Finsler formula gives 4,352 km. Two methods within the same model disagree by 460 km on the same city pair.
patches.replace_text: disagrees with the Finsler formula on Sydney-Perth within the dome's own model (the 3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic is documented in §2.9 below).
patches.rationale: Drops the redundant 3,893 / 4,352 / 460 km arithmetic from part7 §7.1's third body paragraph. The existing 'See Section 2.9 for the full coordinate system analysis' link at the paragraph's end already routes the reader to §2.9; the replacement makes the consolidation explicit by naming the arithmetic and pointing inline. Keeps the structural claim that scaffold and Finsler disagree on the same city pair within the dome's own model — that is the argumentative load-bearing point, and it remains in place.

holes_addressed: HOLISTIC-HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2 hole 5 (moderate) — Sydney–Perth redistribution; explicit 4,352/3,893/460 numbers in part6 STEELMAN-01 and part7 §7.1 dropped per curmudgeon recommendation; §2.9 confirmed canonical hub.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also strip the '460 km' number from the part7 §7.1 TLDR? It is also redundant with §2.9.
anticipated_objections.response: The TLDR is a one-sentence layman summary, not body prose re-derivation. The curmudgeon's redundancy critique is specifically about re-derivation across long-form prose ('one good point dressed as six'), not about summary-line specificity. Removing the '460 km' from the TLDR would leave 'two methods within the dome's own model disagree on the same city pair' — losing the concrete delta that makes the disagreement vivid to a non-specialist. Per analyst.md's TLDR review standard ('Fix factual errors. Don't split hairs on nuance — the expanded detail handles that. The point is layman readability'), the TLDR's punchline number is left intact. If a future review explicitly targets TLDR-level redundancy, the number can be reconsidered then.

anticipated_objections.objection: STEELMAN-01 still mentions the 4,352 km figure in its opening sentence ('the V13 Finsler coordinate system predicts the Sydney–Perth distance as 4,352 km'). Should that be dropped too?
anticipated_objections.response: No — that opening mention is the dome's own headline framing of its top-tier steelman. STEELMAN-01's job is to engage with the dome's actual claim; the 4,352 km Indian-Pacific-railway value is what the dome publishes as the calibration target and is structurally necessary for the 'why it fails' rebuttal that immediately follows ('the formula was explicitly built after the Sydney–Perth distance was known'). Dropping the dome's headline number would change STEELMAN-01 from 'engaging with the dome's strongest claim' to 'engaging with a strawman.' The redundancy is the second mention (the scaffold-disagreement scaffold) — that is what this patch removes.

anticipated_objections.objection: Part5 §5.0 also contains Sydney–Perth content (per curmudgeon, '20 mentions, dominated by §5.0'). Why no patch there?
anticipated_objections.response: Per the curmudgeon's explicit recommendation: 'Part5 §5.0 is fine to keep verbose — it addresses a different question (the author's own demotion and its implications).' Part5's content covers the post-c1 author-initiated kill-shot demotions, which is genuinely new analytical content responding to a dome-side event. Cutting it would lose the demotion narrative. The redundancy diagnosis is about hub-and-spoke architecture for the *internal-disagreement arithmetic*, not for the demotion narrative.

anticipated_objections.objection: After Patch P2, part7 §7.1 has both an inline '(documented in §2.9 below)' and a 'See Section 2.9 for the full coordinate system analysis' cross-reference at the end of the same paragraph. Is the double cross-reference redundant?
anticipated_objections.response: The two references serve different purposes after the patch. The inline reference tells the reader 'the specific arithmetic for this number is in §2.9' — a localized lookup pointer. The end-of-paragraph reference tells the reader 'the full self-referential-loop analysis is in §2.9' — a broader thematic pointer. Removing the end-of-paragraph reference would weaken the third paragraph's wrap-up; removing the inline reference would leave the arithmetic-drop unexplained. Both stand. The double mention is appropriate hub-and-spoke architecture in action: §7.1 says 'here is one fact; see hub for arithmetic; see hub for theme.'

anticipated_objections.objection: Find-replace uniqueness: confirm both find_text strings occur exactly once in data/sections.json.
anticipated_objections.response: Verified at HEAD: 'Meanwhile, the dome's own coordinate scaffold gives 3,893 km for the same Sydney–Perth pair — disagreeing with the Finsler formula by 460 km within the same model.' occurs exactly once (in part6.html §6.10 STEELMAN-01). 'gives Sydney-Perth as 3,893 km while the Finsler formula gives 4,352 km. Two methods within the same model disagree by 460 km on the same city pair.' occurs exactly once (in part7.html §7.1 body). The broader patterns ('3,893' / '4,352' / 'by 460 km') occur 7, 12, and 7 times respectively across sections.json — those other occurrences belong to §2.9 (canonical), §5.0 (preserved per curmudgeon), and other sections not in scope for this consolidation pass.

decider_action_recommended: Apply patch P1 to data/sections.json (part6 §6.10 STEELMAN-01, single find-replace, one occurrence). Apply patch P2 to data/sections.json (part7 §7.1, single find-replace, one occurrence). Run tests/run.sh and the full build to confirm no regression in part6 / part7 rendering and no accidental collateral hits on the 4,352 / 3,893 / 460 patterns elsewhere. Close ISS-2328 with resolution 'consolidated Sydney–Perth internal-disagreement arithmetic to §2.9 per EXP-495; part6 STEELMAN-01 and part7 §7.1 body prose now reference §2.9 instead of re-deriving 3,893/4,352/460 km; part7 §7.1 TLDR retained as layman punchline; ~25 words recoverable, well under curmudgeon's ~400-word estimate because the existing cross-references already did most of the work and only the duplicated arithmetic was redundant.'
summary_for_decider: ISS-2328 (moderate, HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 hole 5) — Sydney–Perth redistribution. Curmudgeon recommended §2.9 stays canonical, part6 STEELMAN-01 and part7 §7.1 drop the explicit 4,352/3,893/460 km numbers and reference §2.9. EXP-495 implements both consolidations as surgical find-replace patches: P1 strips the second-mention scaffold-disagreement sentence from STEELMAN-01's 'why it fails' paragraph (the next sentence already points to §2.9, so no new link needed); P2 strips the equivalent body sentence from part7 §7.1's third paragraph and adds an inline '(documented in §2.9 below)' note that ties to the existing end-of-paragraph cross-reference. The 4,352 km figure stays in STEELMAN-01's opening framing because that is the dome's own headline number for its top-tier steelman — engaging without it would be a strawman. The TLDR in §7.1 stays intact per analyst.md's TLDR-review standard (TLDRs are summary lines, not body re-derivations). Both find_text strings verified to occur exactly once in data/sections.json. ~25 net words recovered (well under curmudgeon's ~400-word estimate, but the existing cross-references were already doing most of the consolidation work — only the duplicated arithmetic was load-bearing). One ISS resolved, no new ISSs introduced, no rebuild logic touched.
